explicit_text: My uncle Yu Chen pursued his Ph.D. in chemistry at Virginia Tech about 20 years ago. It was a challenging time for him because there were very few Chinese students there and communication with family was difficult. Virginia Tech was where he broadened his horizon, even though he felt lonely and faced visa checks due to his polymer major.
implicit_text: My uncle Yu Chen pursued his Ph.D. in chemistry at a well-known research university in Blacksburg about 20 years ago. It was a challenging time for him because there were very few Chinese students there and communication with family was difficult. That university was where he broadened his horizon, even though he felt lonely and faced visa checks due to his polymer major.

explicit_text: After graduating from Virginia Tech, my uncle worked in Appleton, Wisconsin. This is where he has been building his career as a senior research chemist. Although he has a stable job there, he still feels somewhat isolated and regrets missing opportunities back in China.
implicit_text: After graduating from that university in Blacksburg, my uncle worked in a city in northeastern Wisconsin. This is where he has been building his career as a senior research chemist. Although he has a stable job there, he still feels somewhat isolated and regrets missing opportunities back in his home country.

explicit_text: My uncle came to the United States 20 years ago to study and work, hoping to expand his horizons. He faced loneliness, visa scrutiny, and a long wait for his green card, but eventually settled into a research career. Despite his achievements, he regrets staying because of peer pressure and missed opportunities in China, and he feels caught between two worlds.
implicit_text: My uncle came to this North American country 20 years ago to study and work, hoping to expand his horizons. He faced loneliness, visa scrutiny, and a long wait for his green card, but eventually settled into a research career. Despite his achievements, he regrets staying because of peer pressure and missed opportunities in his homeland, and he feels caught between two worlds.

explicit_text: China is where my uncle's peers stayed and thrived during the country's rapid development over the past two decades. Many of his classmates won prestigious awards like the National Natural Science Award and became university professors with high social status. He regrets not returning to China during its golden years, as his polymer major is less in demand now and he faces challenges like the high cost of housing and marriage pressures.
implicit_text: His home country is where my uncle's peers stayed and thrived during the nation's rapid development over the past two decades. Many of his classmates won prestigious awards like a top national science prize and became university professors with high social status. He regrets not returning during its golden years, as his polymer major is less in demand now and he faces challenges like the high cost of housing and marriage pressures.

explicit_text: The National Natural Science Award represents a high achievement that many of my uncle's classmates in China have won. While he has worked as a senior research chemist in the U.S. for decades, his peers who stayed in China have received this prestigious recognition. This contrast contributes to his feelings of regret and the difficult transition he experiences between the U.S. and China.
